english: — śiṅ-zóg (shing zog) W. a rasp. — śiṅ-yzér (shing gzer) a peg. — śiṅ-léb (shing leb) board, plank. — śiṅ-śún (shing shun) the bark of trees. — śiṅ-śéd (shing shed) a rasp.
asa foetida, used as medicine, and (like garlic) as a spice; also n. of a mountain pass between Lahoul and Zaṅkar.

('wooden horse') waggon, cart, carriage, also fig. = tég-pa (theg pa), e.g. śiṅ-rta čén-po (shing rta chen po) frq. in the writings of Tsoṅḱapa; śiṅ-rta-_kór-lo (shing rta 'khor lo) id.; śiṅ-rtai ḱaṅ-bzáṅ (shing rtai khang bzang) the body of a carriage, śiṅ-rtai mda (shing rtai mda) the pole, beam, shaft of a cart, ṕáṅ-lo (phang lo) the wheel, rjes (rjes), lam (lam), śul (shul), srol (srol) the track, rut (of a cart) Cs.; śiṅ-rta rkaṅ-yśig (shing rta rkang gshig) Sch. wheelbarrow; śiṅ-rta-mḱan (shing rta mkhan) Cs. maker of carts, cartwright; śiṅ-rta-pa (shing rta pa) 1. carter, driver, coachman. 2. charioteer.

1. Sch. hazel-nut. — 2. also )yśid-ma (gshid ma), yśid-stón (gshid ston), yśid-záṅ (gshid zang) funeral repast, of which every body may partake; śid-čós (shid chos) religious funeral ceremony; śid-sa (shid sa) Sch. 1. burying ground, cemetery. 2. a fruitful field = yśin-sa (gshin sa). Cf. yśin (gshin).
very, greatly, esp. before adj. and adv., in B. frq.
v. śib (shib).
v. śúb-pa (shub pa) to whisper.
or rú-ru (ru ru) Ld. hip, the fruit of the dog-rose.
Cs. a kind of tree or wood.
with _otón-pa ('thon pa) Cs. to gush out, to stream forth with a noise.
W. to drip through.
a gauze-like texture W.; śil-śil (shil shil) 1. id. 2. Cs.: 'a cant word denoting the noise of any thing'.

good luck, fortune, bliss; de byúṅ-na śis (de byung na shis) if that happens, it will be an auspicious sign, śis-pai miṅ (shis pai ming) a name foreboding good Lt., mi śis-pai ltas (mi shis pai ltas) an omen foreboding ill Wdṅ.; bstán-pai śis (bstan pai shis) acc. to Schl. 232 denotes the religious plays performed in the convents. Cs.: śis(-pa)-po (shis(-pa)-po) one blessed, śis-pa yin-pa (shis pa yin pa) to be blessed, śis-par _ogyúr-ba (shis par 'gyur ba) to become blessed, śis-par byéd-pa (shis par byed pa) to make blessed, to bless; bkra-śis (bkra shis) v. bkrá-ba (bkra ba).

1. acc. to Cunningham and other English authorities the Tibetan word for stag; yet as none of the many Tibetans, from different parts of the country, that were consulted by us, seemed to know this word, it is not unlikely, that in consequence of indistinct hearing it is but a corruption of śa-ba (sha ba) (q. v.). — 2. *śu-śú jhè'-pa* (shu shu byed pa) C. to whistle. — 3. num.: 87.
n. of a plant Med.; Sch.: the rush.

I. sbst. 1. an abscess, ulcer, sore Cs.: śu-ba _otón (shu ba 'thon) an abscess rises, na (na) gives pain, ṕan (phan) heals; *śu-búr* (shu bur) W., and prob. also śu-tór (shu thor) Med, id.; *śu-nág* (shu nag) and bá-śu (ba shu) W. a sore that has become inflamed and rankling. — 2. scab, scurf, scald W. II. vb., pf. (b)śus ((b)shus), fut. bśu (bshu), imp. (b)śu(s) ((b)shu(s)), 1. to take off, pull off, draw off, yzán-gyi gos (gzan gyi gos) to take off a person's clothes, gó-ča (go cha) armour, mtsón-ča (mtshon cha) arms, weapons Pth.; to strip, strip off, e.g. leaves, twigs, págs-pa (pags pa) the skin, the peel, hence (also without págs-pa (pags pa)) to skin, to pare, to peel W., e.g. *'á-lu śú-če* (a lu shu ce) to peel potatoes; gyab-śús (rgyab shus) coat of wool shorn from a sheep, fleece Ld. — 2. to copy, dṕe (dpe) a book, resp. źal-śús byéd-pa (zhal shus byed pa) Cs.; dpe-bśús (dpe bshus) a copied book C.
Ssk., sometimes at the end of books, hail! all hail!
n. of a tract of land in the neighbourhood of Mathura, not far from Agra Wdk.
Tar. 63, 8, prob. also śu-lig (shu lig) Sch., n. of a fabulous country in the north-west.

1. a thrust, push, knock, *śug čém-po jhè'-pa* (shug chem po byed pa) to push off, to give a knock, to elbow, differing from _o_túl-ba ('thul ba) to shove (by a more gentle motion) C. — 2. in comp.: kyo-śúg (khyo shug), v. kyo (khyo); śúg-bza (shug bza) wife, consort, spouse Schr. — 3. W.: old, but still fit for use. — 4. śug-śúg-la (shug shug la) col. for śúb-bur (shub bur) softly, gently, e.g. _o_gró-ba ('gro ba) to walk, to tread etc.
